title: Our Airlines
heroImageAlt: A wide-body airliner at the gate seen through an airport terminal window
intro: FlyAir connects you to hundreds of airlines worldwide — from regional carriers across South Asia to global full-service and low-cost airlines — through one search.

sections.p: SriLankan Airlines, Emirates, Qatar Airways, Etihad, Singapore Airlines, Cathay Pacific, Malaysia Airlines, Thai Airways, IndiGo, Air India, FitsAir, Saudia, Gulf Air, Kuwait Airways, Oman Air, Turkish Airlines and many more operate from Colombo Bandaranaike International Airport (CMB).

sections.h: Full-service vs low-cost — what to expect
sections.p:
FULL-SERVICE CARRIERS (e.g. SriLankan, Emirates, Qatar, Singapore Airlines) typically include checked baggage, meals and entertainment in the fare, offer interline connections for missed flights, and provide cabin classes from Economy through Business and First.

LOW-COST CARRIERS (e.g. AirAsia, IndiGo, FitsAir) sell a lower base fare with most extras unbundled — pick the extras you need (baggage, seat, meal) and pay only for what you use. Their lighter cost structure often makes them the cheapest option on short and regional routes.

We show the inclusions of each fare in plain English before you pay, so you can compare like-for-like.

Many international itineraries involve codeshares — a flight marketed by one airline but operated by another. We display BOTH the marketing carrier (the one you book with) and the OPERATING carrier (the one whose aircraft and crew you fly with), because baggage allowances, meals and seat selection follow the operating airline.

Most full-service fares earn miles with the operating carrier’s loyalty programme; some discounted classes earn at a reduced rate or none at all. The fare brand label tells you which mileage accrual class applies — paste your number at booking and we pass it through to the airline.

Each airline (and often each fare brand within an airline) sets its own baggage allowance, change fee and cancellation rule. We surface these on every fare result so you can choose the option that fits your trip.

Wide-body long-haul aircraft (Airbus A330/A350/A380, Boeing 777/787) typically offer more cabin classes, lie-flat business seats on overnight routes, and a wider entertainment selection. Regional narrow-bodies (Airbus A320/A321, Boeing 737, ATR 72) operate the bulk of short-hop routes around South Asia and the Gulf.
